channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Panama Canal chief dismisses Trump’s claims on China, fees
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority chief dismissed President-elect Trump’s claims that China is controlling the trade route and criticized his comments about fees. Ricaurte Vásquez Morales told The Wall Street Journal that Trump’s accusations that China is running the Panama Canal are “unfounded.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostage found dead in Gaza, military says
item.description: Israeli forces have recovered the body of a hostage held in Gaza, the military said Wednesday, adding that it was identifying additional remains that could belong to another captive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González is rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano And Astrid Suárez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: More Americans jailed in Venezuela pose a test of Trump's deal-making foreign policy
item.description: As many as 10 Americans are behind bars in Venezuela as President Nicolas Maduro prepares to be sworn in for a third term.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Goodman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 Americans arrested in Venezuela on eve of Maduro inauguration over ‘terrorism’ claims
item.description: Two Americans have been arrested in Venezuela, with President Nicolas Maduro claiming the charges relate to an intent to "practice terrorism against the Venezuelan people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeffries: 'Confused' by Trump's 'obsession' with Greenland
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) hammered President-elect Trump on Wednesday for what he characterized as an “obsession” with the idea of the U.S. taking over Greenland, accusing Trump of fixating on an issue of no consequence for working-class Americans.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Imprisoned Belarus activist resurfaces after being held incommunicado for over 700 days
item.description: An imprisoned opposition leader in Belarus has resurfaced in a video shot by a pro-government blogger after over 700 days of no contact with his family.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich: Trump 'trying to project' strength with Greenland talk
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) says President-elect Trump is attempting to project strength with his recent remarks about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark, comments that have not been well-received across the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Tristan Lejeune, The Hill

item.title: Bolton: Trump's Greenland gambit could help Xi, Putin
item.description: John Bolton, President-elect Trump's former national security adviser, said Trump’s unwillingness to rule out military force on Greenland could bolster the positions of Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin as they seek to justify their expansionist visions.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israeli Army says it recovered body of hostage in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli Army on Wednesday said it had recovered the body from the Gaza Strip of an Israeli citizen who was kidnapped by Hamas during its terrorist attack on Oct. 7 and held hostage, and was working to identify the remains of another body as a possible Israeli hostage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: House Republican: Gulf of Mexico name less important than Trump's actions
item.description: Rep. Austin Scott (R-Ga.) said President-elect Trump’s musing about changing the name of the Gulf of Mexico to the Gulf of America is less important than his actions in the region, particularly when it comes to curbing illegal fishing and drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 23-year-old hiker found after surviving for 2 weeks in Australian mountain range
item.description: A medical student from Melbourne who was missing in a remote Australian mountain range for two weeks survived on two muesli bars, foraged berries and creek water, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump expected to appoint Eric Trager as Middle East director for National Security Council
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to announce Eric Trager as Senior Director for the Middle East and North Africa on the National Security Council, a congressional aide told The Hill. Trager has served as a professional staff member for Republicans on the Senate Armed Services Committee since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany's leader says all countries must respect existing borders, in a rebuttal of Trump
item.description: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz says the principle of inviolability of borders applies to every country, however powerful.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tents arrive for survivors of a quake that killed 126 in freezing, high-altitude Tibet
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Relief teams in western China shifted their focus to resettling survivors after a search Wednesday for any remaining victims of a deadly earthquake that struck a day earlier near a holy city for Tibetan Buddhists.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis has named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, choosing an Italian nun
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Monday named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, appointing an Italian nun, Sister Simona Brambilla, to become prefect of the department responsible for all the Catholic Church's religious orders.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: Ukrainian officials say that a Russian missile attack in the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia has killed at least 13 civilians and wounded about 30 others.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader says his son-in-law has been kidnapped in Caracas
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González, who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election, said his son-in-law was kidnapped Tuesday in Venezuela's capital.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 9 miners are trapped in a coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state
item.description: Indian authorities have summoned the army to help rescue at least nine workers who are trapped inside a flooded coal mine in the country's northeastern Assam state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump reinforces 'all hell will break out' if hostages not returned by inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump once again threatened 'all hell will break out' if the hostages in Gaza are not freed by the time he enters office, though he refused to detail what actions he will take.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants left stranded in troubled resort as Mexico disperses them far from US border
item.description: ACAPULCO, Mexico (AP) — About 100 migrants from various countries wandered directionless and disoriented through the streets of the troubled Pacific coast resort of Acapulco.
item.dc:creator: ANTONIO CASTILLO and MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senegal and Chad say ousting of French troops was their sovereign decision
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Senegalese and Chadian leaders said that the ousting of French troops from their territories was their sovereign decision, rejecting remarks from French President Emmanuel Macron that suggested otherwise.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and BABACAR DIONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who was Dietrich Bonhoeffer? An AP Explainer about the anti-Nazi pastor
item.description: Who was Dietrich Bonhoeffer? Dietrich Bonhoeffer was a Lutheran pastor and theologian from Germany executed by the Nazis in 1945 at age 39 for his role in a resistance movement that included attempts to assassinate German leader Adolf Hitler.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law student killed by elephant during vacation to Thailand: officials
item.description: Blanca Ojanguren, a 22-year-old student from Spain, was killed by an elephant on Friday during a bathing session at Koh Yao Elephant Care
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 killed in West Bank 'shooting spree' including Israeli police officer: reports
item.description: A shooting spree by Palestinian terrorists in the West Bank has left three Israelis dead as a manhunt is ongoing for the suspects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation
